package com.fusion.crypto;

import com.fusion.telemetry.Telemetry;
import com.fusion.core.Guard;

import javax.crypto.KeyAgreement;
import javax.crypto.SecretKey;
import javax.crypto.spec.SecretKeySpec;
import java.security.*;
import java.security.spec.ECGenParameterSpec;
import java.util.Random;

/**
 * Fusion Hybrid Engine (Java Edition)
 * -----------------------------------
 * The core cryptographic implementation of the Fusion 50/50 Standard.
 * * <p><strong>Design Philosophy:</strong></p>
 * <ul>
 * <li><strong>Binding Property:</strong> The session key is mathematically bound to both the ECC and Lattice secrets.</li>
 * <li><strong>Downgrade Resistance:</strong> The engine will reject any handshake that is missing the PQC component.</li>
 * <li><strong>Observability:</strong> All operations are instrumented with the Telemetry system.</li>
 * </ul>
 * * @author Fusion Security Team
 * @version 2.0.0 (Integrated)
 */
public class HybridEngine {

    private KeyPair classicalKeyPair;
    private MiniKyber.KeyPair pqcKeyPair;

    /**
     * Initializes the cryptographic engine.
     * Generates a fresh P-256 KeyPair and a MiniKyber Lattice KeyPair.
     * This operation is tracked by Telemetry.
     */
    public void init() {
        Telemetry.measure("HybridEngine:Init", span -> {
            // 1. Classical: NIST P-256
            KeyPairGenerator kpg = KeyPairGenerator.getInstance("EC");
            kpg.initialize(new ECGenParameterSpec("secp256r1"));
            this.classicalKeyPair = kpg.generateKeyPair();
            
            // 2. Post-Quantum: Module-LWE
            this.pqcKeyPair = MiniKyber.generateKeyPair();
            return null;
        });
    }

    public PublicKeyBundle getPublicKeys() {
        return new PublicKeyBundle(this.classicalKeyPair.getPublic(), this.pqcKeyPair.publicKey());
    }

    /**
     * Encapsulates a shared secret for the remote party.
     * * @param remoteKeys The public keys of the recipient.
     * @return A result containing the AES Session Key and the Ciphertext to send.
     * @throws SecurityException if the remote keys violate the Hybrid Standard.
     */
    public HybridResult encapsulate(PublicKeyBundle remoteKeys) {
        
        // --- GUARD RAIL ---
        // Prevents Downgrade Attacks by enforcing structure presence.
        Guard.validateHybridStructure(remoteKeys.classical(), remoteKeys.pqc());

        // --- MEASUREMENT ---
        // Captures the latency delta between Classical and Quantum ops.
        var result = Telemetry.measureHybrid("Fusion:Encapsulate",
            // Classical Task (ECDH)
            () -> {
                try {
                    KeyAgreement ka = KeyAgreement.getInstance("ECDH");
                    ka.init(this.classicalKeyPair.getPrivate());
                    ka.doPhase(remoteKeys.classical(), true);
                    return ka.generateSecret();
                } catch (Exception e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
            },
            // Quantum Task (Lattice Encap)
            () -> MiniKyber.encapsulate(remoteKeys.pqc())
        );

        byte[] classicalSecret = result.classical();
        MiniKyber.EncapResult pqcResult = result.quantum();

        // --- FUSION KDF ---
        // K_session = SHA-256( K_classical || K_quantum )
        try {
            MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
            digest.update(classicalSecret);
            digest.update(pqcResult.sharedSecret());
            byte[] fusedKeyBytes = digest.digest();

            return new HybridResult(
                new SecretKeySpec(fusedKeyBytes, "AES"),
                new CiphertextBundle(null, pqcResult.ciphertext())
            );
        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("SHA-256 missing in runtime");
        }
    }

    // --- Records ---
    public record PublicKeyBundle(PublicKey classical, MiniKyber.PublicKey pqc) {}
    public record CiphertextBundle(PublicKey classicalEphemeral, MiniKyber.Ciphertext pqcCipher) {}
    public record HybridResult(SecretKey sessionKey, CiphertextBundle ciphertext) {}

    /**
     * MiniKyber: A functional simulation of Crystal-Kyber (ML-KEM).
     * Uses small parameters (n=4) for demonstration but correct algebra.
     */
    public static class MiniKyber {
        private static final int Q = 3329;
        private static final int N = 4; 
        private static final SecureRandom RNG = new SecureRandom();

        public record PublicKey(int[][] A, int[] t) {}
        public record PrivateKey(int[] s) {}
        public record KeyPair(PublicKey publicKey, PrivateKey privateKey) {}
        public record Ciphertext(int[] u, int v) {}
        public record EncapResult(Ciphertext ciphertext, byte[] sharedSecret) {}

        public static KeyPair generateKeyPair() {
            int[] s = sampleNoiseVector();
            int[][] A = generateRandomMatrix();
            int[] e = sampleNoiseVector();
            int[] t = vecAdd(matVecMul(A, s), e);
            return new KeyPair(new PublicKey(A, t), new PrivateKey(s));
        }

        public static EncapResult encapsulate(PublicKey pk) {
            int[] r = sampleNoiseVector();
            int[] e1 = sampleNoiseVector();
            int e2 = sampleNoise();
            int[] u = vecAdd(matVecMul(pk.A(), r), e1); 
            
            // Encode message bit
            int bit = RNG.nextBoolean() ? 1 : 0;
            int message = bit * (Q / 2);
            int v = (dot(pk.t(), r) + e2 + message) % Q;
            if (v < 0) v += Q;
            
            return new EncapResult(new Ciphertext(u, v), new byte[]{(byte) (bit == 1 ? 255 : 0)});
        }

        // Math Helpers
        private static int sampleNoise() { return RNG.nextInt(5) - 2; }
        private static int[] sampleNoiseVector() {
            int[] v = new int[N];
            for(int i=0; i<N; i++) v[i] = sampleNoise();
            return v;
        }
        private static int[][] generateRandomMatrix() {
            int[][] m = new int[N][N];
            for(int i=0; i<N; i++) for(int j=0; j<N; j++) m[i][j] = RNG.nextInt(Q);
            return m;
        }
        private static int[] vecAdd(int[] a, int[] b) {
            int[] r = new int[N];
            for(int i=0; i<N; i++) r[i] = (a[i] + b[i]) % Q;
            return r;
        }
        private static int dot(int[] a, int[] b) {
            int sum = 0;
            for(int i=0; i<N; i++) sum += a[i] * b[i];
            return sum % Q;
        }
        private static int[] matVecMul(int[][] A, int[] v) {
            int[] r = new int[N];
            for(int i=0; i<N; i++) r[i] = dot(A[i], v);
            return r;
        }
    }
}
